Hey, I also notice the bridge between the two connector on the side of the board. I cannot find the diagram he is referring too. Does anyone know where to find it ? thank you in advance. 
A man who just want to repair his beloved headsets.

author: @paradoxic1888
text: My daughter spilled coffee on my DT 990's and now only the right ear cup works, with the left one being very soft and no bass. Why did this happen and can it be fixed? Please tell me it can, I just bought them this month and it would heartbreaking to throw them in the trash.

author: @tonytweaker
text: Hello! I have a problem with my DT990 Pro's. Out of nowhere left ear stopped working. I opened it , and it looks fine. no bridge's no loose cables. so now i wonder what could it be? maybe the jack i somehow faulty. any help will be much appreciated :) Greetings

replies.author: @solcjamaicangrooves6584
replies.text: I have exactly the same problem as you with my DT990 Pro's. Everything is well connected but left ear stopped working.

replies.author: @tonytweaker
replies.text: @solcjamaicangrooves6584 i assume It is a problem with the cable. Cable costs about 20$ but i don't have a person WHO can solder the cable for me :(

replies.author: @yaago
replies.text: so sad, the same happened to me out of nowhere, I was listening to music just fine yesterday but today the right earcup won't work

author: @ismaelzaheer9495
text: i bought a pair, stopped working after 2-3 months, so i bought another stopped working after a week :( so annoyingggggggg

author: @snow5391
text: i was listening to spotify on full blast on these and the left ear just went out...

replies.author: @Kid420
replies.text:
Same here, I’m thinking of trying to send them for repair but I know it would take time to ship it back and forth and I’m kinda scared of trying to fix it myself..
I wish someone warned me of this popular problem with these headphones before buying them.

replies.author: @friendlybadger4823
replies.text: so what did you guys do? this just happened to me

replies.author: @snow5391
replies.text: @friendlybadger4823 idk there’s nothing that necessary can be done… i’ve tried fixing them by taking them apart but no luck. been sitting in my drawer ever since.

replies.author: @TT-df9hp
replies.text: Stupid idea to listen to them full blast. 1) because of your ears and 2) the risk of damaging your headphones. Hopefully you've learnt your lesson.

author: @ruxvioz6479
text: Yes luke good to see your doing well for yourself

author: @martinwinfield2935
text: How about seeing what you have actually done. How about testing the channels to check that they are the correct way around. May help if you stop  swinging your arms around too.

author: @IrishFuryan
text: After this happened to my first set of headphones. I bought same again, but this time using string wrapped around both sides of every connection, and then applied two part epoxy over the string..

author: @scotttovey
text: Did you pull a bit of extra wire into the case and tie it in a knot or put a clamp on it so that doesn't happen again?

author: @lnwolf41
text: If you're going to keep doing this type of video, you might want to ACTUALLY  show how you did it, not just say here's the problem,  fade to black, then, "I fixed the problem", now it works.

replies.author: @martinwinfield2935
replies.text: As  they say a little knowledge can be dangerous.

author: @truthseeker3907
text: Good Job and Thumbs up! 👍

author: @kimkeam2094
text: Most of my life I have done exactly what you are doing, and it is amazing how many “friends “ will drop by and ask you to do a small job for them. I found as I got older and injured from work, that they nearly all dropped me off their list when I couldn’t help them. I have learned that helping true friends unconditionally is great, but all the others should pay an amount to keep you going, whether in cash or a bartered agreement, trade or swap. It keeps the relationship honest and you won’t be used. Keep the good work up!

author: @martinX74
text: Nice fix. I've got some bt headset to fix myself
